Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Appearance Of Defendant At Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NEIL FOSTER's Motion To Compel Appearance Of Defendant At Deposition.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The report and recommendation of Judge Pro Tem Borrowman is adopted in full.

Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of the PMK for M.A. Silva Holdings, Mr. Manuel Silva, is GRANTED IN PART. The parties are to complete the deposition of Mr. Silva via remote means as Mr. Silva is a resident of Portugal. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $5,000 to reflect the partial success on the motion. Sanctions payable by July 7, 2022.

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/SRB)

MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 C.C.P. to Judge ETHAN P. SCHULMAN
Off Calendar Jun-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NEIL FOSTER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 C.C.P. to Judge ETHAN P. SCHULMAN.

Plaintiff Neil Foster's "motion for peremptory challenge per 170.6 CCP to Judge Ethan P. Schulman" is denied. A challenge is authorized only to a judge "before whom the action or proceeding is pending, or to whom it is assigned." (Code Civ. Proc. 170.6(a)(2).) The instant action is not pending before or assigned to Judge Schulman. No appearances are required.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT J. KAHN, Esq. of Huguenin Kahn LLP (Tel: 916-367-7098) for plaintiff Neil Foster;
Atty. STACEY A. VILLAGOMEZ, Esq. of Allen Matkins Leck Gamble Mallory & Natsis LLP (Tel: 213-622-5555) for defendant M.A. Silva Holdings, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NEIL FOSTER's Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Neil Foster's motion for order granting leave to file second amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy favoring liberal amendment of pleadings. Defendants do not show that they would be prejudiced by the amendment: no trial date has been set, discovery remains open, and no dispositive motions have been filed. If the addition of the Portugese defendants and service on those parties presents an obstacle to the timely prosecution of the action, defendants may raise that issue in connection with the assignment of a trial date and/or by other appropriate motion. The Court declines defendants' request that it condition its ruling granting plaintiff leave to file the amended complaint on granting them an unspecified "extended amount of time" to respond.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/EPS)
